journal_entry.reason: Cash borrowed from bank creates a loan liability. The 3-year term and 6% interest rate with semi-annual payments establish the loan terms, but at inception only the principal is recorded.

output.liability_increase_reason: Notes Payable (or Bank Loan Payable) increases by $200,000 when Atlas borrows cash from the bank. This is one liability account increasing.
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity accounts are involved. This is a borrowing transaction, not an owner investment or retained earnings transaction.
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is earned. Borrowing cash creates a liability obligation, not revenue.
output.asset_decrease_reason: No assets decrease on the credit side. Cash increases (debit side), but no asset is given up or reduced.
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividends or owner drawings are involved in this borrowing transaction.
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses are decreased. The interest is payable semi-annually in the future; at the time of borrowing, only the principal liability is recorded. No expense reversal occurs.

output.asset_increase_reason: Cash increases by $200,000 from the bank loan proceeds received.
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividend distributions in this transaction.
output.expense_increase_reason: No expenses incurred. Interest expense will be recognized later when it accrues, not at loan inception.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid down or extinguished. A new liability is being created (credit side).
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are reduced. This is a borrowing transaction, not an equity transaction.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue accounts are affected. Borrowing does not involve revenue.

output_tax_specialist.reasoning: Transaction is a bank loan borrowing. No tax is mentioned in the text. Loans and financing transactions are not taxable supplies under tax law.
